text: The surgeon uses a suture to string together multiple fibroids, bringing the suture through the skin to secure them during the laparoscopic myomectomy.
The surgeon uses a laparoscopic tenaculum to securely grasp the fibroids during the myomectomy procedure.
The surgeon brings the suture out of the abdomen, securing all the fibroids in one place to facilitate other tasks such as suturing.
The surgeon prepares to remove the fibroids by bringing in the sutures and tying them together, ensuring all fibroids are secured for removal.
The surgeon places the fibroids into a containment bag within the abdominal cavity and proceeds to remove them using power morcellation.
